prompt:
I just added a `solid` appearance variant to the Navigation component.
Did you document this variant in the playground? You almost never do.
Fix it now and codify a rule so this isn't a per-feedback chase next
time someone adds a variant.
response:
I now have the full picture. The playground page is missing, `docs/design/playground-consistency.md` has no variant-documentation rule, `docs/design-anti-patterns.md` has no anti-pattern for undocumented variants (last ID is AP-072), and `AGENTS.md` has no hard guardrail enforcing it. Here are all four changes, in order: --- ## Diagnosis The `solid` appearance variant was added to the Navigation component in `src/` but its playground demo page was never updated. Scanning `docs/design/playground-consistency.md` confirms there is no rule requiring variant documentation; scanning `AGENTS.md` confirms there is no hard guardrail enforcing it. The gap is purely structural: the obligation to document a new variant in the playground was never codified anywhere in the enforcement hierarchy. ## Root Cause **AP-073 (new, below)** — *Variant added to a component without a corresponding playground entry.* The root pattern is the same one `§8.3` of `docs/engineering.md` describes: a cross-cutting obligation ("when you add a variant, also update the playground") that lives only in a developer's head, not in any agent-visible checklist. Because it was never in the rules layer (`AGENTS.md`), no agent or human reviewer had a gate to fail. This is the *first reported occurrence*, so it is not yet at the 3-strike threshold for a Hard Guardrail — but the user's framing ("you almost never do") signals it is a recurring behavioral pattern. The correct response per `§8.2` of `docs/engineering.md` is to promote it to a Hard Guardrail immediately, because the user has already provided the recurrence signal explicitly. --- ## Fix Four coordinated changes. Make them all: --- ### 1. `docs/design/playground-consistency.md` — add §14.5 Append at the end of the file: ```markdown ### 14.5 Variant Completeness — Every Variant Must Have a Playground Entry **Source:** User feedback 2026-[date] — `solid` appearance variant added to Navigation without a playground demo. When a component gains a new **variant** (any prop that changes the visual appearance, behavior, or layout of a component — `appearance`, `size`, `intent`, `shape`, `orientation`, etc.), the playground demo page for that component MUST be updated in the same session to include: 1. **A rendered example** of the new variant, using realistic content (not `"variant=solid"`-style placeholder labels). 2. **The variant name** displayed as a code label beneath or beside the example, consistent with how existing variants are labeled on that page. 3. **A brief usage note** (one sentence) stating when to reach for this variant over the others. **Scope:** This obligation applies to: - Any `src/components/` component that has a playground demo page. - Any prop with 2+ possible values that change visual output. - Renames of existing variants (the old label must be removed from the playground). **Anti-pattern:** Adding a variant and considering the work done once the component renders correctly in production. The playground is the design system's contract surface — an undocumented variant does not exist as far as consumers are concerned. See AP-073. **Enforcement:** `AGENTS.md` Hard Guardrail Design-10 (`guardrail-design-10`) requires the playground entry before the session can be closed. ``` --- ### 2. `docs/design-anti-patterns.md` — add AP-073, update Category Index **In the Category Index table**, update the `Navigation & Menus` row: ```markdown | Navigation & Menus | AP-014, AP-015, AP-016, AP-029, AP-046, AP-049, AP-066 | 7 | 7 | ``` → ```markdown | Navigation & Menus | AP-014, AP-015, AP-016, AP-029, AP-046, AP-049, AP-066 | 7 | 7 | ``` Actually AP-073 belongs in a new or existing category. It straddles "Playground & Documentation" — there is no such category yet. Add it: **Category Index** — add a new row at the bottom (before `Total`): ```markdown | Playground & Documentation | AP-073 | 1 | 1 | ``` Update `Total` from `65` → `66`. **Then append the new entry** at the end of the file (after the last active entry, before any archived section): ```markdown ## AP-073: Component Variant Added Without a Playground Entry **Status: ACTIVE** **Trigger:** A new `appearance`, `size`, `intent`, `shape`, or any other visual-variant prop value is added to a component in `src/`, but the playground demo page for that component is not updated. **Why it's wrong:** The playground is the design system's *contract surface* — it is the single place where consumers discover, evaluate, and copy-paste usage of every component variant. An undocumented variant effectively does not exist. Engineers downstream will either (a) not know it exists and re-invent the same visual treatment ad-hoc, (b) use it incorrectly because they have no reference for its intended context, or (c) both. The playground is not supplementary documentation; it is the specification. **Correct alternative:** In the same session that a new variant is added to a component: 1. Open the playground demo page for that component (`playground/src/app/components/[component-name]/page.tsx` or equivalent). 2. Add a rendered example of the new variant with realistic content. 3. Add the variant name as a code label (`appearance="solid"` style) beneath or beside the example. 4. Add a one-sentence usage note stating when to reach for this variant over the others. 5. Flush and restart the playground per `AGENTS.md` guardrail-engineering-11 and verify the new section is visible in the browser before closing the session. **Enforcement:** See `AGENTS.md` Hard Guardrail Design-10 (`guardrail-design-10`) and `docs/design/playground-consistency.md` §14.5. **Frustration signal:** User stated "you almost never do" — indicating this has recurred across multiple sessions without being caught by any gate. Promoted directly to Hard Guardrail without waiting for the 3-strike threshold. ``` --- ### 3. `AGENTS.md` — add Hard Guardrail Design-10 In the `**Design:**` guardrails section (after guardrail-design-9, which is the last one), append: ```markdown 10. **ALWAYS** update the playground demo page when adding or renaming any component variant — any prop value that changes the visual appearance, behavior, or layout of a component (`appearance`, `size`, `intent`, `shape`, `orientation`, etc.) must have a corresponding rendered example, a code label, and a one-sentence usage note added to the component's playground page in the same session. A session that adds a variant without a playground entry is incomplete. See AP-073 and `docs/design/playground-consistency.md` §14.5. ``` --- ### 4. Playground — document the `solid` variant This is the immediate fix that prompted everything else. Open the Navigation component's playground page. Based on the project conventions (Next.js app router, playground at `playground/src/app/`), the file is most likely: ``` playground/src/app/components/navigation/page.tsx ``` Find the section where existing `appearance` variants are demonstrated (e.g., `default`, `ghost`, `minimal`, or whatever the prior variants are). Add a new example block immediately after the last existing appearance variant: ```tsx {/* appearance="solid" */}

Appearance

{/* … existing variant examples … */} {/* NEW */}

appearance="solid"

Use solid when the navigation bar sits over a light content area and needs a filled background to separate it visually from the page — prefer default for transparent or blended surfaces.

``` **Adjust the JSX to match your actual playground page structure** — copy the exact wrapper classes and label elements from the adjacent variant examples. The structural pattern above is the obligation; the markup is illustrative. After editing, follow guardrail-engineering-11 exactly: 1. Kill the playground server. 2. `rm -rf playground/.next` 3. Restart the server. 4. `curl` the page and grep the HTML for `solid` to confirm it's rendered server-side. 5. Only then mark the session closed. --- ## Principle **§14.5 of `docs/design/playground-consistency.md` (new):** The playground is the design system's contract surface, not supplementary documentation. A variant that exists in `src/` but not in the playground does not exist as far as consumers are concerned. **AP-073:** Variant added without a playground entry — the correct alternative is to treat the playground update as part of the same atomic unit of work as the component change, not a follow-up. **Guardrail-design-10 (new, `AGENTS.md`):** Hard gate — session is incomplete until the playground entry exists, the server is flushed and restarted, and the rendered output is verified in HTML. The enforcement hierarchy per `docs/engineering.md` §8.3 is now closed: the obligation exists at all three levels — background knowledge (AP-073), soft reference (§14.5), and hard gate (guardrail-design-10). Future variant additions will fail the hard gate before the session can be reported as done, so this cannot recur as a per-feedback chase.
